title: Pattu Poochi lyrics Aanandha Thaandavam Song lyrics
stanzas:
Song: Pattu Poochi
Singer: Naresh Iyer, Harmony
Music: GV Prakash
Lyrics :  Vairamuthu

Pattu poochi kootai pidithathu
vatta siragal paanai eduthathu
innoru siragal ennai kudithathu
sukku sukkai nooru sukku sukkai

oru sorga thundai naanum kaana
sokki sokki aanmai naana
pachai ratham patri yerindhathu
ekku thappai ayyo ekku thappai

iru kulai thalliya vaazhai maram
en mele saaindhathu moondru tharam
thavame seyyamal thavanai illamal
devadhai thandathu kadhal varam

pattu poochi kootai pidithathu
vatta siragal paanai eduthathu
innoru siragal ennai kudithathu
sukku sukkai nooru sukku sukkai

oru sorga thundai naanum kaana
sokki sokki aanmai naana
pachai ratham patri yerindhathu
ekku thappai ayyo ekku thappai

chinnadhaga oru ichai vandhathadi munnale
andha ichai indru oru imsai aanathadi unnale

en moolayin kadhirellam indrodu azhinthathadi
kan kaana azhagellam kadal pola padinthathadi

kandu ondrada kaatrin kai theendi
kadhalin boogampathu arindhadi

indru indru ennai nindru konnadhadi pennazhagu
adi kondru kondru uyir mendru thinnathadi un azhagu

unai enni unai enni uyirellam veengukiren
vizhi paartha azhagellam viral paarka yengukiren
kanne nee konda kannil mai thottu kadhalin mudhal kavi yezhudukiren

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Pattu Poochi?

The song Pattu Poochi conveys themes of love and nostalgia, reflecting on cherished memories and emotions.

What is the plot context of the movie Aanandha Thandavam?

Aanandha Thandavam explores deep emotional connections and the journey of self-discovery, making the song's themes resonate with the narrative.

Who is the singer of Pattu Poochi?

The song is sung by the talented Naresh Iyer, known for his melodious voice and emotive singing style.

Who wrote the lyrics for Pattu Poochi?

The lyrics were penned by the renowned lyricist Vairamuthu, celebrated for his poetic expressions in Tamil cinema.

When was Pattu Poochi released?

The song was released on April 6, 2009, as part of the movie Aanandha Thandavam.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Pattu Poochi, the listener is taken on a melodic journey that encapsulates the essence of love and cherished memories. The song beautifully complements the narrative of Aanandha Thandavam, where emotions run deep and connections are explored. With Naresh Iyer's captivating vocals and Vairamuthu's poignant lyrics, the song evokes a sense of nostalgia and happiness. It serves as a reminder of the simple joys found in love and relationships.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the enchanting vibes of Pattu Poochi from the film Aanandha Thandavam, where Naresh Iyer's soulful voice beautifully captures the essence of love and nostalgia. This song, penned by the legendary Vairamuthu, resonates with listeners, making it a timeless favorite. Dive into the melodious journey that intertwines heartfelt lyrics with captivating rhythms.
stanzaMeanings: The singing insect caught in a circle, the round wings taken, another wing has drunk me, sweet sweetness, a hundred sweet sweetness.
I am a heavenly messenger, feeling sad and sad, I have a green blood that has spilled, oh what a mistake, oh what a mistake.
The banana tree with two bunches has fallen on me, without doing three things, without giving me a boon of love, the god has not come.
The singing insect caught in a circle, the round wings taken, another wing has drunk me, sweet sweetness, a hundred sweet sweetness.
I am a heavenly messenger, feeling sad and sad, I have a green blood that has spilled, oh what a mistake, oh what a mistake.
A small desire has come, earlier that desire is now a joy because of you.
All my sorrows have vanished today, the beauty I see is like the sea.
I found a way to hold the wind, the sweetness of love is known.
Today, today, I was scolded, the beauty of a woman has taken my life, your beauty.
Thinking of you, thinking of you, I surrender my life, seeing your beauty, I am drawn to you.
Oh dear, you touched my eyes, I write the first poem of love.
